result:
The hum of the refrigerator is a constant companion these days. It’s a low thrum, a white noise backdrop to the quiet of my apartment. Lately, this quiet is filled less with the anticipation of plans and more with the gentle ache of introspection. I've been doing a lot of thinking, a lot of sifting through the layers of "this" and "that" to try and understand the now.

The world feels heavy. News cycles churn with a relentless force, and the weight of it all seems to press down, demanding attention and inciting worry. I try to find pockets of peace, moments where the worries dissolve into the sunlight streaming through my window or the warmth of a mug of tea. 

Reading has become my solace. I lose myself in the stories, in the imagined lives and far-off lands. It’s a temporary escape, a journey inward and outward simultaneously. I find myself gravitating towards books that explore themes of resilience, of finding beauty in the midst of turmoil. Maybe it’s a reflection of my own desire to navigate the current climate with a sense of hope, a gentle refusal to let the anxieties completely consume. Maybe it’s just the comfort of a well-told tale. Either way, the hum of the refrigerator and the turning of pages, are my current companions.

Reading has become my solace. I lose myself in the stories, in the imagined lives and far-off lands. It’s a temporary escape, a journey inward and outward simultaneously.
